---
title: "CV"
---

<div class="pub-buttons" style="margin-bottom: 2rem;">
<a href="RJM-CV-v.oct25.pdf" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" download>
  Download CV (PDF)
</a>
</div>

## Education

::: {.cv-section}

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
July 2011
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
**Ph.D.**, Political Science, The University of Iowa

Dissertation: *Strategic Oversight and the Institutional Determinants of Legislative Policy Control*

Committee: Frederick J. Boehmke (Chair), Douglas Dion, Christian B. Jensen, Raymond G. Riezman, and Charles R. Shipan (University of Michigan)
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2004
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
**B.A.**, Political Science & Philosophy, Saint Peter's College
:::
:::

:::

## Academic Positions

::: {.cv-section}

::: {.cv-institution}
**George Mason University** — Fairfax, Virginia
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2025 -- present
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
**Director of Political Science and Public Policy PhD Programs, Schar School**
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2017 -- present
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
**Associate Professor, Schar School**
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2018 -- 2021
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
**Director of Undergraduate Programs, Schar School**
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2014 -- 2017
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
**Assistant Professor, Schar School**
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2011 -- 2014
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
**Assistant Professor, Department of Public and International Affairs**
:::
:::

::: {.cv-institution}
**University of Michigan** — Ann Arbor, Michigan
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2013 -- 2015
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
**Robert Wood Johnson Foundation Scholar in Health Policy Research**
:::
:::

:::

## Publications

::: {.cv-section}

[View full publications list](/publications.html){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}

### Book Reviews

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2025
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
Review of Steven Rogers's *Accountability in State Legislatures* for *Publius*, Volume 55, No. 2 (Spring 2025)
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2020
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
Review of Ben Merriman's *Conservative Innovators* for *JPART*, Volume 30, No. 2 (April 2020)
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2016
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
Review of James M. Curry's *Legislating in the Dark* for *The Journal of Politics*, Volume 78, No. 2 (April 2016)
:::
:::

:::

## Awards & Fellowships

::: {.cv-section}

### Research Awards

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2015
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
*State Politics and Policy Quarterly Award* (State Politics and Policy Section, APSA) for "Experts, Amateurs, and Bureaucratic Influence in the American States," with Graeme Boushey
:::
:::

### Teaching Awards

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2023
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
Outstanding Teaching Award, Schar School of Policy and Government
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2022
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
OSCAR Mentoring Excellence Award, George Mason University
:::
:::

### Grants & Fellowships

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2016 -- 2019
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
NSF Collaborative Research (RIDIR): "DAPPR: Diffusion Analytics for Public Policy Research," with Frederick J. Boehmke, Bruce A. Desmarais, William W. Franko, Jeffrey J. Harden, Yu-Ru Lin, and Jason Windett. Oct 1, 2016 -- Sep 30, 2019. $808,128
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2013 -- 2015
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
Robert Wood Johnson Foundation Scholars in Health Policy Research Fellowship (University of Michigan site)
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2012
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
"The American Governor: Politics, Policy, Power and Leadership" Research Grant, Center on the American Governor -- Eagleton Institute of Politics (Rutgers University), with Jon C. Rogowski and Josh M. Ryan. $4,980
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2009
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
Graduate College Summer Fellowship, University of Iowa
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2009
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
NSF award to attend EITM summer institute, University of Michigan
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2008
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
NSF award to attend EITM summer institute, Washington University in St. Louis
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2005
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
Foreign Language and Area Studies (FLAS) Summer Fellowship, Consortium in Latin American and Caribbean Studies at UNC-Duke
:::
:::

::: {.cv-timeline}
::: {.cv-timeline-date}
2004 -- 2010
:::
::: {.cv-timeline-content}
Political Science Graduate Fellowship, University of Iowa
:::
:::

:::
